details.description: Nighttime street view of a long, ornate French colonial–style government building with cream/yellow stucco walls and red tile roofs, richly decorated with pilasters, pediments, and sculpted reliefs. A central belfry tower rises above with balconies and is topped by the Vietnamese national flag. The façade features an arcaded ground floor with wrought‑iron gates and balustraded balconies above, illuminated by warm white and teal architectural lighting that accentuates the details. Potted topiary trees line the front. Motorbikes and cars move along the broad plaza/boulevard in front under a dark sky.

details.description: Night景 photograph of a grand French colonial–era government building with a prominent clock tower, located on a long colonnaded façade. The structure is illuminated with warm yellow lights that highlight arches and ornate columns, and teal accent lighting emphasizes decorative detailing along the upper levels. A flag flies atop the central tower. The building features a series of rounded arches on the ground floor, balustrades, sculptural ornamentation, and a red-tiled roof, suggesting Beaux-Arts/French colonial influences. Street activity is visible in the foreground with cars and motorbikes along Le Thanh Ton / Le Thánh Tôn area, all set against a dark night sky.

details.description: Nighttime street scene in central Ho Chi Minh City showing the ornate French-colonial city hall illuminated with warm white and teal lighting. The long facade features arched arcades at ground level, paired columns, sculptural reliefs, balconies, and red-tiled roofs. A tall central tower rises with a flag at the top. Potted topiary plants line the building, and motorbikes and cars pass along the street in the foreground.

details.description: A night photograph of a grand French colonial-era civic building with a clock tower and an arcaded façade. The long façade is illuminated in warm white and teal lights, highlighting columns, arches, and decorative detailing. A red flag flies atop the tower. In the foreground, cars and motorbikes pass along the street, while pedestrians and potted trees line the entrance to the building. The structure is located in Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam, near Le Thanh Ton Street in District 1.

details.description: A vertical nighttime photograph capturing the majestic façade of the Ho Chi Minh City Hall (People's Committee Building) in Vietnam. The building is illuminated with warm yellow lights on the lower levels and cool teal or green accent lighting on the upper architectural details and roofline. The structure exhibits classic French Colonial architecture with a cream and yellow exterior, white ornamental reliefs, Corinthian columns, and a series of arched windows and walkways. A prominent central tower with a belfry rises on the right side, topped by a Vietnamese flag fluttering against the black sky. The perspective extends down the length of the building's wing to the left. In the foreground, the street (Lê Thánh Tôn) appears wet or reflective, mirroring the red taillights of cars and motorbikes. Several scooters with riders are visible driving away from the camera, and a row of potted topiary plants lines the sidewalk in front of the building's arched arcade.

details.description: A night photograph of the Ho Chi Minh City People's Committee Building, a prominent example of French Colonial architecture. The building features a cream-colored facade with intricate decorative reliefs, sculptures, and a central clock tower topped with a flagpole flying the Vietnamese flag. It is dramatically illuminated with warm yellow and cool green lights. In the foreground, motorbikes and cars are visible on Lê Thánh Tôn street, with their headlights and taillights creating streaks of light. The sidewalk in front of the building is lined with manicured potted topiary trees.

details.description: Nighttime street-level view of a grand French-colonial/Beaux‑Arts municipal building illuminated with warm and teal floodlighting. The cream-colored facade features repetitive arched arcades, Corinthian pilasters, ornate pediments with sculptural reliefs, wrought‑iron gates, and a central clock/bell tower topped by a Vietnamese flag and a red‑tile roof. The long symmetrical wings extend down the boulevard. Potted topiary lines the frontage, while scooters and cars pass along a wide paved boulevard. The scene matches the facade and tower of Ho Chi Minh City Hall facing Nguyễn Huệ Walking Street in District 1, Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam.

details.description: A grand, brightly illuminated French colonial-style building at night, with a long colonnaded arcade on the ground level featuring arched openings and decorative gates. The facade is richly ornamented with statues, pilasters, and balustrades, and is accented by teal-green lighting along the columns. A prominent tower with a clock rises above the corner, and a red flag atop the mast is visible. The street in front shows lighted vehicles and motorbikes, with potted shrubs lining the walkway, creating a ceremonial urban atmosphere.

details.description: Nighttime street scene of a grand, French-colonial era civic building illuminated with warm and teal lights. The facade features arched arcades, balustraded balconies, pilasters, sculptural reliefs, and red-tiled mansard-style roofs. A central tower with a small belfry and balcony rises above, flying a red flag. Potted topiary shrubs line the frontage, while scooters and cars move along the broad plaza/ boulevard in front under a dark sky.

details.description: Night-time exterior of a grand, multi-story French colonial–style municipal building. The façade features a long colonnaded arcade with arched windows, ornate sculptures, and decorative reliefs. A tall clock tower rises at one end, topped with a flag, while warm interior lighting and teal exterior uplighting highlight the architectural details. The structure sits along a wide, paved street with parked cars and motorbikes in the foreground, giving a sense of a bustling urban setting after dark.

entities.name: French colonial city hall-style government building
entities.notes: Clock tower, arched colonnade, ornate sculptures, decorative reliefs; teal uplighting and warm interior lighting.

details.description: A vertical night photograph capturing the illuminated facade of the People's Committee Building (formerly Hôtel de Ville) in Ho Chi Minh City. The French Colonial architecture is dramatically lit with warm yellow and cool cyan lights, highlighting the central bell tower, the clock face, the intricate relief sculptures, and the arched loggias supported by Corinthian columns. A Vietnamese flag flies at the very top of the spire against a pitch-black sky. At street level, the wet pavement of Lê Thánh Tôn Street reflects the red taillights of passing cars and motorbikes, adding a dynamic urban element to the scene. The perspective is angled from the street, looking up towards the building's central and left wings.

details.description: A night view of the Ho Chi Minh City Hall, also known as the People's Committee Building, in Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam. The building is a grand example of French colonial architecture, featuring a symmetrical facade with a central clock tower, ornate sculptural reliefs, and multiple arched windows and balconies. It is dramatically illuminated with warm yellow and cool teal spotlights. The Vietnamese national flag is visible atop the central tower. In the foreground, the street shows light traffic with motorbikes and cars, their lights reflecting on the dark pavement. Potted topiary plants line the sidewalk in front of the building's arched ground floor.
